title: RISK: broadcast() ignores send errors; no delivery failure tracking
description: relay/src/state.rs line 108: broadcast() collects all send futures and awaits them with join_all(), but every send error is silently ignored (let _ = tx.send(m).await). If a client's channel fills (unlikely but possible under load), that client never receives the broadcast. If a malicious client holds the channel without reading (backpressure), they receive nothing while others get the message. No delivery confirmation, no failed-client list, no operator visibility. A solo maintainer sees 'client says they never got message X' and has no diagnostic data. Risk: blind broadcast failures; no delivery accountability.
close_reason: False positive. CLAUDE.md §8: broadcast ignores send errors is correct — a disconnected client's channel close is treated as clean exit. state.rs broadcast() ignoring send errors is load-bearing correct behavior.
